raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$81.00} by XXXX XXXX at XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) for what they described as a luggage fee. I am disputing this charge because I was traveling on a XXXX-issued ticket ( confirmation XXXX ) that already included one standard carry-on bag at no additional cost. I did not have any checked baggage. 

At the XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was told I could not board unless I paid this additional luggage fee. I paid under pressure so I could board my return flight to New York. On my outbound flight from New York to XXXX with the same ticket and fare class, I was not charged any luggage fee, so the return-leg charge was inconsistent and unexpected. 

I opened a billing dispute with Citi ( Dispute ID XXXX ) explaining that the fee was improper and not consistent with the fare conditions. Citi first requested more documents, which I providedincluding my XXXX ticket, fare rules, and evidence that no fee was charged on the outbound flight. Citi then closed the case stating my correspondence was conflicting, claiming I had said the charge was unauthorized, even though I clearly stated that the charge was authorized but improperly assessed under duress.

I am requesting CFPBs assistance in having Citi review this dispute correctly as a billing dispute for an improper charge, not a fraud dispute. I believe the bank mishandled my claim by focusing on a technical classification error instead of reviewing the merits of the case and the evidence I provided. I am seeking reversal of the {$81.00} charge and a fair review of the documentation.
